title: AI research loop closes in a day as Karpathy returns to pre-training - TCR 05/20/26
description:
Google DeepMind's Co-Scientist and FutureHouse's Robin system closed the full biomedical research loop in roughly a day of machine time across two Nature papers, Andrej Karpathy joined Anthropic to work on pre-training after leaving OpenAI in 2024, and Alibaba's T-Head subsidiary shipped 560,000 units of its Zhenwu M890 inference chip with 144 GB of on-package memory.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-20-2026/

0:00 Sunrise bedside sleep apnea sensor cleared by FDA
1:41 Multi-agent AI closes the full research loop in a day
5:25 Karpathy returns to pre-training at Anthropic
8:32 Representational drift and the fluid brain
11:38 Alibaba Zhenwu M890 closes a substitution path
14:24 Close

This episode covers the Co-Scientist and Robin multi-agent research demonstrations in acute myeloid leukemia and dry age-related macular degeneration, Karpathy's return to frontier pre-training at Anthropic, the FDA clearance of Sunrise's bedside sleep apnea sensor for definitive diagnosis, accumulating evidence of representational drift reshaping how neuroscience understands memory, Alibaba's 560,000-unit Zhenwu M890 shipment paired with Qwen3.7-Max, and the D.C. Circuit's questioning of the AI Safety Institute supply-risk designation against Anthropic.

Co-Scientist runs a debate-style architecture in which multiple Gemini agents propose, critique, and refine candidate hypotheses; Robin runs a chained pipeline with specialized agents handling stages a human team would divide among postdocs. Both arrived at novel candidates that ranked plausibly against expert assessment and held up in wet-lab validation. The labor between a question being asked and a candidate being ready for the bench compressed from months to roughly a day, with the wet-lab work now the gating step. Karpathy's move into pre-training places one of the field's most recognizable communicators inside the layer where the next generation of frontier models gets built. Sunrise's rechargeable mandibular sensor cleared the FDA for definitive multi-night at-home sleep apnea diagnosis, moving a billion-person diagnostic gap from the specialty clinic to the bedside table. A Nature feature pulled together a decade of evidence that individual neurons reshuffle which memories they encode over days and weeks while the memories themselves persist - reframing the substrate of cognition as fluid where the textbooks assumed it was fixed. Alibaba's T-Head subsidiary shipped 560,000 Zhenwu M890 inference parts with 144 GB on package and released Qwen3.7-Max tuned to the stack end to end, closing a substitution path U.S. export controls had assumed would stay open. A D.C. Circuit panel pressed government counsel on whether the AI Safety Institute's supply-risk designation against Anthropic was substantially based on the company's protected safety speech.

Additional Reading:
- Nature: Accelerating Scientific Discovery With Co-Scientist: https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-026-10644-y
- Nature: A Multi-Agent System for Automating Scientific Discovery: https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-026-10652-y
- The Algorithmic Bridge: Andrej Karpathy Joins Anthropic: https://www.thealgorithmicbridge.com/p/andrej-karpathy-joins-anthropic-what
